titleStmt.title: SII 17.696: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 696. (A.R. No. 640 of 1904.) RĀMAGIRI, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple.

This inscription records the gift of the village Nīrvāyil, made free of levies (āyam) in cash, gold and paddy by Śaśikulachaḷukki Tani-niṉṟu-veṉṟa Vīranārasiṅga alias Yādavarāya for taking out the deity Tirukkārikarai-uḍaiyār in procession during Chittirai festival.

Vīra Nārasiṅgadēva alias Yādavarāya, a well-known feudatory of Kulōttuṅga III figures as the donor in this inscription and hence the king may be identified with Kulōttuṅga III. The donor is mentioned with a string of birudas such as Karavāḷa-Bhairava, Śrī-Kāḷahastīśa-pādārādhaka, Kanuppārapuravarādhīśvara, etc.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevarkku yāṇṭu [3 10 2] vatu samadhigata pañcamahāśabda mahāmaṇḍaleśvara veṅgivallabha godāviridīra kanuppārapuravarādīśvara karavāḷa bhairava śrīmat śrīkāḷahastiśa pādārādhaka parabalasādhaka prāpta samasta vidyāvicārabhūṣaṇa saptarājāśraya śaśikula cāḷuki taniniṉṟu veṉṟa viranāraciṅka
nāna yātavarāyanen jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu naṭuviṉmalai niṉṟaiyūr nāṭṭu Uṭai yār] tirukkārikaraiyuṭaiyārkku Eṉ perāl cittirait tirunāḷ Eḻuntaruḷivikka Itukku tevatānamāka nirvāyil kācāyam poṉṉāyam nellāyam Uṭpaṭa viṭṭeṉ vira nāraciṅka tevanen Itai māṟṟuvān keṅkaiyiṭai kurāṟ pacuvai koṉṟa brammavattip paṭuvār .
Digital edition of SII 17.696 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
